outlook.headline: AI keeps DRAM tight, but momentum is easing
outlook.summary: Fundamentals still read strong: AI-driven DRAM spend, HBM mix, and next-node transitions are keeping supply tight and supporting tools, test, and memory vendors like Micron, Applied Materials, Lam Research, FormFactor, and MKS. But the mention trend has cooled from a recent peak to 2.5x, and a few reads are only neutral or pass-through, suggesting the narrative is still positive but no longer accelerating. Watch whether tight supply and AI pull continue to offset any pushback from high prices or DRAM reductions in downstream devices.

drivers.label: AI-driven DRAM demand remains strong
drivers.detail: Multiple companies cited hot DRAM demand tied to AI/AI compute and data-center needs, supporting continued spending.

drivers.label: Supply stays constrained by node and capacity limits
drivers.detail: Reads point to tight supply, HBM mix, and limited capacity growth, keeping the market favorable for suppliers.

drivers.label: Next-node transitions are still ramping
drivers.detail: 1C, DDR5, LPDDR5, and HBM/advanced-memory adoption are driving more tool and content demand.

drivers.label: Mention momentum has cooled from the recent peak
drivers.detail: Company mention frequency is still elevated at 2.5x, but down ~10% versus the trailing average, implying less incremental excitement near-term.

drivers.label: High DRAM prices are starting to pressure some end uses
drivers.detail: At least one read framed shortages and expensive DRAM as forcing customers to reduce DRAM usage rather than expand it.

risks: DRAM prices/supply tightness ease faster than expected, reducing urgency in supplier reads.
Downstream customers begin redesigning around DRAM scarcity, limiting unit growth.
The mention trend continues to fade, signaling the AI/DRAM story is losing incremental momentum.
